SHORT.Item: **CLEARED-STALE 2026-07-10 (swarm, zero code)**: the double-pop fix already shipped inside #197's settleAfter + deferred-deliver machinery; solo-skip + solo-cancel scenarios both green
SHORT.Source: PR #144 finding, evidence in swarm audit

SHORT.Item: **Post-progression workflow unclear (any experience level).** After adding a suggested progression, the Solo choice prompt (Save & open Studio / Skip) + the "Solo" button both appear - a new user is curious about Solo, felt they "can't cancel", and had no clear read on the next step. **"Can't cancel" FIXED in PR #197**: the Solo modal gained a visible **Cancel** and dismiss gestures (backdrop/Escape/Back) now stay on Compose instead of navigating into the Studio. REMAINING (needs a vision call): the broader intended after-you-build flow - should Solo/Save auto-surface at all, what is the ideal next-step guidance for a new user.

**Compose delete-handle overflows the chord card (1-3 chords).** In the `full` diagram-card stage, the per-slot `x` remover floats above/outside the card bounds (disconnected from the chord it deletes). Contain it to the card. **FOLLOW-UP FIXED 2026-07-10 (S-DELHANDLE-CENTER, keypicker branch, v142)**: the x glyph was padding-nudged off the circle's center - content box now carved to the exact 24x24 circle + flex-centered, true by construction at every stage; prog-delete-handles scenario green.

**4-6 chords should fit with NO vertical scroll - "so close to fitting".** The fill-row/grid stages nearly fit the fixed region at 5-6 chords but tip into scroll. Tighten card/gap sizing so 4-6 fit above the fold (A7 geometry gate at 412x915). **ADDRESSED (PR #198), needs phone confirm**: trimmed the fixed .prog region (min-height 88->78, padding 12->9, full-stage gap 10->8) to reclaim ~16px WITHOUT shrinking the diagram cards (phone-DPI floor forbids that). Can't verify the fit at 412x915 on the remote box - tap the branch preview to confirm 4-6 fit with no scroll; if still short, the fallback is compacting the 4-chord stage to tokens.

**All-view Maj/Min/7th selected chip is full accent CTA - reads as friction.** The selected type-filter chip is `.chip.on` (accent fill, D-SELECTED-ACCENT). Operator feels it shouts. THIS IS THE D-SELECTED-ACCENT reconsideration flagged in the visual-language design: a secondary FILTER's selected state competing with the one primary. Decision needed: reverse D-SELECTED-ACCENT (selected = outline, fill reserved for primary) app-wide, or a filter-specific quieter selected treatment.

**After Clear, the view stayed on a stale "All" pin instead of returning to In-key.** With D-DEFAULT-C the initial state is In-key on C major, but Clear did not reset the view pin - if the user had tapped All while composing, that pin persisted over the fresh canvas. FIXED: Clear now resets chordView to follow-the-key + rebuilds the palette -> In-key on the default key.

**Suggested next-chords need a visual distinction from the full palette.** The theory-ranked "NEXT CHORD" suggestions and the full In-key grid currently look alike. Differentiate by EMPHASIS + ORDER (music-theory-coach ranks by resolution strength; visual-language gives the suggestions a hair more prominence - a recommended marker/tint - while the full palette stays browse), never a new hue.

**Studio solo-scale picker should be theory-aware + emphasis-laddered.** Landing in the Studio (Solo practice, C major from a Compose progression), the "SOLO OVER IT" picker (Ionian / Pent major / Pent minor / Blues) defaults to **Pent minor** - wrong best-choice for a MAJOR-key context (Ionian or Pent major is the natural home over major). Per operator: (1) default-select the theory-BEST scale for the incoming key+mode, (2) OUTLINE the other picks (visual-language emphasis ladder - one filled primary), (3) disable/remove incompatible options where applicable. Best-scale + compatibility logic to come from the new **music-theory-coach** skill. **FIXED (PR #198)**: the picker now default-selects the theory-best pentatonic (major-family key -> Pent major, minor-family -> Pent minor, a Blues key keeps its own blues scale) via the music-theory-coach rule, and does a full select() so the fretboard/notes/guide render that scale too (not just the chip highlight). Part 2 (outline others) is the existing chip emphasis ladder - the one selected chip fills, the rest stay outline. Part 3 (disable incompatible) is n/a: over a diatonic key all four scale choices are playable (major/minor pentatonic interplay is a feature, not an incompatibility), so nothing to disable. **EXTENDED (PR #198)**: (a) the default is now PROGRESSION-AWARE - `Tracks.inferSoloDefault(key,mode,seq)` reads the actual chords via `Circle.romanFor` and upgrades the default to Mixolydian (a bVII-major tell over a major key) or Dorian (a major-IV tell over a minor key), else the safe pentatonic. (b) Mixolydian + Dorian are now SELECTABLE context chips (major key -> +Mixo +Dorian, minor key -> +Dorian, deduped against the key's own mode, none on a Blues key) - their `SoloGuide.card` coaching already shipped. Chip visual + fit need a phone tap on the branch preview. **SKIP-PATH FIX (PR #198)**: the Compose Solo->Skip hand-off (songbook.js) dropped the progression's `seq` (passed only key+mode), so progression-aware inference could never fire on the Skip path - the exact path UAT used. Now Skip carries `progression.slice()` as seq, matching the Save path.

**A note/chord spelled with a SHARP contradicts the degree label the same UI shows (music-theory-coach REJECT).** Building C-F-A# in C major, the third chord shows "A#" under a "bVII" roman-numeral label. Music-theory-coach verdict: over a stated key, spelling is FUNCTION-driven - the bVII is the lowered 7th (B lowered = **Bb**), and "A#" names a raised 6th (#6), the WRONG function. Showing "A#" while labeling it "bVII" is internally self-contradictory (numeral says flat-7, note says sharp-6). Coach REJECTS whenever the UI asserts a key/degree function; sharp spelling is acceptable ONLY in a keyless/chromatic context. This is the FORK-4 canonical-sharp limitation - the fix is key-aware spelling, which is exactly what the stalled linchpin **#98** (`spellScaleKeyAware`/`keyLabel`) delivers. Operator (2026-07-10) AUTHORIZED removing FORK-4 -> key-aware, confirming it is deterministic. **CORE BUILT + PROVEN (PR #198)**: `Circle.spellScaleKeyAware(root,mode)` + `Circle.spellRootInKey(keyRoot,keyMode,noteRoot)` - deterministic letter-per-degree spelling (each of A-G once, accidental to hit each pitch). 14 passing unit tests (`test/key-spelling.test.js`): F major -> Bb (not A#), bVII of C -> Bb, the note name now AGREES with the degree label. REMAINING (the staged wiring = the real FORK-4 removal): (1) route the render paths (chord names, scale note list, fretboard) through the key-aware spellers instead of `spell()`/`spellScale()`; (2) the picker offers SHARP-only key names (A#, D#, G#), and those keys spell to double-sharps under correct theory - so the removal also needs the picker to name flat keys with FLAT letters (Bb not A#). That picker change is a scope/UX call (operator owns). Supersedes the #98 approach with a tested core.

Persona pair proves both sides on pixels; journey state (one-tip-at-a-time) modeled via dismissNotables.

**G2 (75) SHIPPED (USDD arc 2, red->green)**: beginner-gated `postprog` notable fires the moment the progression goes Solo-ready ('Your chords are ready - tap Solo to practice over them'), never a modal; tapping Solo IS the goal so it self-retires (dismiss + slot clear), releasing the one-tip journey slot for the Studio tip - the full beginner journey (cue -> Solo -> modal -> Studio tip) is one pixel-proven scenario. Resolves S-POSTPROG-FLOW's remaining vision half; taste-reviewable on the preview.

**RESOLVED as S-KEYPICKER-PREFERRED 2026-07-10 (branch claude/keypicker-preferred-names, v142 - PR pending connector re-auth)**: operator answered IV-2 by directive ("key selector drift - shows sharps only. SSOT") - every key selector now displays preferred key names from the ONE provider (Circle.preferredTonicName): Compose root grid, compact chip, key readout heading, repertoire-form Key dropdown, triads-page chips (on its own branch). **FIXED 2026-07-10 (branch claude/triads-key-spelling, v140 - PR pending GitHub connector re-auth)**: Triads & Inversions kept its own sharp-only NAMES table from before the FORK-4 retirement, so F major showed "IV = A#" instead of Bb (operator UAT). All displayed chord/note names now route through Circle.noteInKey (theory SSOT, no duplicate speller); tokens (key chips, deep-links, storage) stay canonical-sharp. Regression scenario triads-key-spelling.json (shape assert: no A# anywhere in an F-major cycle).

**SHIPPED 2026-07-10 (branch claude/triads-audible, v143)**: inversion chord cards are tap-to-hear buttons - a tap strums the card's exact fullFrets voicing from the ACTIVE profile's open-string frequencies (accurate on every supported instrument by construction; shared ChordAudio engine, keyboard-reachable role=button). S-RN-STYLE folded in: standalone I/IV/V numerals in prose carry the .roman mono+accent family so they never blend into sentences (operator UAT).

**Compose progression delete needs protection + undo (operator UAT 2026-07-10: "too easy to delete a chord... offer 1 (or 2?) levels of undo using simple mechanism tracking previous actions").** Design intent: no confirm() (feedback-debt direction is REMOVING those) - reuse the app's existing undo-toast pattern (Toast.showAction, the setlist item-remove undo) + a small bounded action stack (1-2 levels) for progression deletes. SHIPPED 2026-07-10 (branch claude/delete-undo, v144): first x tap ARMS (red, 1.6s auto-disarm, movement-cancelled taps intact), second deletes; bounded 2-level remove-undo stack on the Clear-undo toast pattern; deletes keep the stack, every other mutation invalidates it via the A3 choke point (keepRemoveUndo param). Unit suite locks "first tap must NOT delete"; prog-delete-undo scenario proves arm -> delete -> toast -> Undo -> restore on pixels.

**PROTOTYPE SHIPPED 2026-07-10 (branch claude/prog-reorder, v145)**: drag-and-drop chord reorder across all 3 strip stages (2D nearest-center targeting so grid6's second row works). Touch = 300ms long-press lifts (early movement = scroll, untouched); mouse = lift on movement. Accent insertion edge marks the drop side; commit splices + full A3 invalidation; trailing click swallowed so a drop never strums. Live-probe finding: the delete-x 44px hit area covers a compact chip's center - drags may START on it (gestures separable: rm tap is movement-cancelled). Touch FEEL is the operator's pass.

**SHIPPED 2026-07-11 (#235, v150)**: setlist edit-mode remove handle joins the ONE inline-remove grammar (S-DELETE-UNDO) - quiet at rest, first tap ARMS red (1.6s auto-disarm, gate at the list-item PRIMITIVE), second tap removes, undo banner kept; J2 rest-red retired (red-at-rest reserved for labeled .btn.danger). Red-first scenario setlist-remove-arm + consistency-lint rule + source lock.

LZ HANDED OFF: ps-internal packet (ccp handoffs/cockpit-lz-packet-20260711.md) -> spawned autonomous session building FastAPI + schema spine behind AAD (notifies operator directly).**

**SHIPPED 2026-07-11 (audit + 4-lane fix swarm, PRs #230-#233, v146-v149).** Read-only audit ([ui-reconcile-audit-20260710.md](ui-reconcile-audit-20260710.md)): 14 keyed-surface spelling misses + 8 primitive divergences, with a correctly-keyless guard list. Fixes at PRIMITIVES: Lane A song-view/Stage view-local speller (the screenshot bug - chip row/sheet/Stage/transpose/Maximize respell, no-double-transpose guarded) + delete button = danger primitive + .full class seam; Lane B Library facet chips/row badges/form Key dropdown (keyRank order preserved, values stay tokens); Lane C Studio prose/jam query/scale-tip mode/relative-key names (circle.js kept pure); Lane D 9 radii snapped to role tokens + 10 lint rows + the .actions .btn.full seam rule. DEFERRED (registered): chord-chip primitive unification (.chordChips .c vs .bt-st-chordchip - markup change, follow-up), native confirm() dialogs (SETX phase 2), .li-up/.li-dn setlist keys (findings-register rider).

**Friction (infra, diagnosed 2026-07-11): BOTH overnight ps-internal spawn sessions died at repo-attach with zero pushes.** Root cause: fresh-session triggers fired into the Cloud-With-Config environment (env_018ePk6pcuQ3xVYDS2dKausA), whose pre-attached nhruska sources lock the session's owner tier BEFORE its first add_repo - so `add_repo problemsolutions/ps-internal` dies on the cross-owner v1 limit at STEP 0 (triggers trig_014Ey2aRwZ2JzncgUnVkKu9S 09:05Z + trig_01RpyWGkzriwEhDvLyPLcge8 11:42Z, both `run_once_fired`, pushed_at never moved off 2026-07-07). The corrected path (spawn into the Default env, env_011CUMCmi2MSZxFpRc21acMx, no pre-attached repos) needs a permission approval the autonomous surface could not deliver ("permission stream closed" x4, including an AskUserQuestion). Encoded lesson: **cross-owner missions must spawn into an environment with NO pre-attached sources for the other owner tier** - the Default env, or a purpose-made problemsolutions env. Operator surfaced via 🔴 telegram (msg 10); mission packet stays live in ccp handoffs/cockpit-lz-packet-20260711.md.

**SHIPPED 2026-07-12 (UAT burst pair, #249 v157 + #250 v158)**: the Library + is a true 3-way setlist picker per the operator's bursts. #249: chordless rows get a GHOST + at the list-item primitive (dashed/faint, reason as aria-label, explaining toast) - an empty add slot never ships. #250: a row with a KNOWN key but no chords gets a LIVE + that seeds the key's tonic chord through the existing record machinery (updateCustomItem / the openEditOrAdd promotion shape - matchKey merge, no duplicate rows) and adds to the set; ghost narrows to keyless+chordless only. Scenarios setadd-keyseed + setadd-evident (fixture-seeded) + regressions green. DEFERRED (vision candidate, operator-gated): setlist-jam-items - chord-less tracks addable AS jam items (Perform learns to play tracks, not just sheets).

SHORT.Item: **Friction (infra, 2026-07-12): fresh-session spawns in the Default env die MID-RUN.** Attempts 3 (trig_013hDfzZgXTWZRfFwhQvUnpy, 13:50Z) and 4 (trig_01RiqP8WimPtyE8WSiaiuZwb, 20:18Z, adopt-mode) both attached ps-internal, pushed a branch + draft PR within ~7 min, then went permanently silent (attempt 4 despite an explicit 15-min push-cadence order; last pushes 13:57Z / 20:34Z). Signature: repo-attach + early pushes succeed, session dies ~10-15 min in - cause not diagnosable from the orchestrating session (no transcript access). 4-attempt cap enforced; operator holds the recovery (own session adopting the claude/cockpit-lz branch - 🔴 telegram msg 12). Encoded lesson: **long autonomous cross-owner missions need a HUMAN-attached session or a purpose-built env until spawned-session stability is proven; the adopt-the-branch guard made attempts cheap and work cumulative.**

**Friction (agent-side, self-caught 2026-07-11 overnight): the parent's coupled-step commit landed on the LOCAL main checkout** because `git worktree add` for a branch already checked out in the agent's worktree fails silently under `

**Friction (surface-side, operator-flagged 2026-07-11): the Claude Android app DROPPED a typed operator message** - his preferences burst ("using metaphors in lyrics... compounded from previous sessions") never arrived and had to be re-sent via screenshot. Cost: one operator round-trip at ~1AM. Not fixable app-side from here; mitigation encoded: treat screenshots-of-typed-text as first-class burst input (already practiced), and the queues.json interview surface remains the durable channel for anything long.

**Friction: prescribed testing steps were not intuitive or possible.** The agent's "build C, Bb, F" test steps failed the operator: (a) Bb is non-diatonic to C so it's not offerable from the suggested/In-key palette (had to discover the All view + that Bb renders as A#), and (b) the agent had not traced the Skip path end-to-end, so the test could not have passed anyway (seq was dropped - see S-SOLO-SCALE-DEFAULT fix). Mobile-dev-coach lesson: never prescribe a manual test the agent has not traced as executable ON THE ACTUAL SURFACE and data-path. Encode: verify the code path carries the data before writing the test steps; write steps in the app's own vocabulary (All view, A# not Bb).
